name: Augustus Loop
description: Augustus Loop is a disting NT implementation of one of Expert Sleepers' oldest products, the VST plug-in of the same name. Essentially, it's a tape-inspired stereo delay. The delay time can be dialled in manually, or set by tap tempo or a clock input. The longest delay possible is around 44 seconds. Being a tape delay, you can change the tape speed via CV. Patching an LFO into here is your route to all manner of subtle detuning or extreme mangling effects. You can also stop and reverse the tape. There is an option to run the delay signal through an 'effects loop', allowing you to insert other effects or processing into the guts of the delay. Note that the 'Pitch CV' input changes the tape speed. It is labelled pitch rather than speed to emphasise the fact that it is scaled 1V/octave. There are four 'tape read heads' with independent delay times and stereo positions, allowing for straight stereo delays, ping pong delays, or hybrid multi-tap style effects.
gui_description: The display shows the feedback amount bottom left and the delay time bottom right. The centre area shows an animation indicating the tape direction and speed. If using a clock input, the word ‘Clock’ flashes up every time the algorithm receives a clock pulse. The word ‘Tap’ is displayed if the tap tempo function will set the delay time on the next tap i.e. it shows whether tap tempo is ‘live’.

parameters.name: Time (coarse)
parameters.description: Sets the delay time. The coarse and fine delay times are added to produce the actual delay time.

parameters.name: Time (fine)
parameters.description: Sets an adjustment to the delay time, in milliseconds. The coarse and fine delay times are added to produce the actual delay time.

parameters.name: Delay multiplier
parameters.description: A multiplier to apply to the delay time set by the parameters, the tap tempo, or the clock.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.description: The overall delay feedback amount.

parameters.name: L-L Time
parameters.description: Scales the delay time of the left-to-left feedback path, as a percentage of the overall delay time.

parameters.name: L-R Time
parameters.description: Scales the delay time of the left-to-right feedback path, as a percentage of the overall delay time.

parameters.name: R-L Time
parameters.description: Scales the delay time of the right-to-left feedback path, as a percentage of the overall delay time.

parameters.name: R-R Time
parameters.description: Scales the delay time of the right-to-right feedback path, as a percentage of the overall delay time.

parameters.name: L-L Level
parameters.description: Scales the amount of the delayed left signal mixed into the left feedback path.

parameters.name: L-R Level
parameters.description: Scales the amount of the delayed left signal mixed into the right feedback path.

parameters.name: R-L Level
parameters.description: Scales the amount of the delayed right signal mixed into the left feedback path.

parameters.name: R-R Level
parameters.description: Scales the amount of the delayed right signal mixed into the right feedback path.

parameters.name: Mono-ize
parameters.description: Reduces the stereo width of the incoming signal. At zero the signal is reduced to mono, at a pan position set by the 'Initial pan' parameter.

parameters.name: Initial pan
parameters.description: Sets the pan position of the mono-ized signal. -100 is fully left; 100 is fully right.

parameters.name: Dry gain
parameters.description: The amount of the dry signal to mix into the outputs. At “-40” there is no dry signal at all i.e. it's actually –∞dB.

parameters.name: Effect gain
parameters.description: The amount of the effect (delay) signal to mix into the outputs. At “-40” there is no effect signal at all i.e. it's actually –∞dB.

parameters.name: Input level
parameters.description: Attenuates the input signal fed to the tape write head.

parameters.name: Pitch inertia
parameters.description: Sets the amount of 'inertia' or slew on the pitch CV input. At zero, the tape speed follows the pitch input closely; at the maximum value, pitch changes are quite gradual.

parameters.name: Stop tape
parameters.description: When on, the tape speed is set to zero. Note that the Pitch inertia affects how quickly the tape stops and starts.

parameters.name: Reverse tape
parameters.description: When on, the tape is reversed. Note that the Pitch inertia affects how quickly the tape reverses.

parameters.name: Bit depth
parameters.description: Controls the bit depth used in the delay memory (note, not the bit depth used in any other processing). Setting this to '0' (16 bit) doubles the maximum delay time.

parameters.name: Inertia free
parameters.description: Enables 'Inertia free' mode. When activated, the algorithm crossfades between the old and new delay times.

parameters.name: Inertia fade time
parameters.description: The fade time to use when in Inertia free mode.

parameters.name: Pitch LFO speed
parameters.description: Sets the speed of the pitch modulation LFO.

parameters.name: Pitch LFO depth
parameters.description: Sets the depth of pitch modulation by the LFO.

parameters.name: Clear loop
parameters.description: When on, instigates a rapid clear of the delay buffer (while maintaining passthrough of the dry signal).

parameters.name: Filter type
parameters.description: Sets the filter type.

parameters.name: Filter freq
parameters.description: Sets the filter frequency.

parameters.name: Filter Q
parameters.description: Sets the filter resonance.

parameters.name: Saturation enable
parameters.description: Enables the saturation processing (on the tape output, before the filter).

parameters.name: Saturation
parameters.description: Sets the depth of the saturation effect, by applying gain before the saturation waveshaper.

parameters.name: Saturation shape
parameters.description: Controls the shape of the saturation. At '100' the effect is that of soft saturation and clipping. At '0' the effect is of hard digital clipping.

parameters.name: Tap tempo
parameters.description: When this parameter transitions from 0 to 1, the algorithm acts on a tap tempo event.

parameters.name: Clocks required
parameters.description: Sets the number of consistent clocks required to change the delay time.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector.

parameters.name: FX Loop position
parameters.description: Enables the effects loop and sets in position the signal flow.

parameters.name: FX Loop output L
parameters.description: Sets the left output to the effect loop.

parameters.name: FX Loop output R
parameters.description: Sets the right output to the effect loop.

parameters.name: FX Loop input L
parameters.description: Sets the left input from the effects loop.

parameters.name: FX Loop input R
parameters.description: Sets the right input from the effects loop.

input_ports.name: Pitch CV input
input_ports.description: CV input to change tape speed, scaled 1V/octave.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: CV input used to set delay time based on the interval between rising edges.

input_ports.name: FX Loop input L
input_ports.description: Left input from the external effects loop.

input_ports.name: FX Loop input R
input_ports.description: Right input from the external effects loop.

input_ports.name: Pitch CV input
input_ports.description: The CV input to use for pitch.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The CV input to use as the clock. The delay time is set as the time between two rising clock edges.

input_ports.name: Left input
input_ports.description: Sets the bus for the left input.

input_ports.name: Right input
input_ports.description: Sets the bus for the right input.

output_ports.name: FX Loop output L
output_ports.description: Left output to the external effects loop.

output_ports.name: FX Loop output R
output_ports.description: Right output to the external effects loop.

output_ports.name: Left output
output_ports.description: Sets the bus for the left output.

output_ports.name: Right output
output_ports.description: Sets the bus for the right output.
